title: Flying Domestic in NZ with Kids — Air NZ Tips, Baggage, and Seat Selection
meta_desc: Discover expert tips for flying domestic in NZ with kids on Air NZ, including baggage allowance and seat selection
intro: Flying domestic in NZ with kids can be a daunting task, but with the right tips and tricks, you can make the journey stress-free. Air NZ offers a range of options for families, including family seating and generous baggage allowance. In this article, we'll explore the best ways to navigate domestic flights in NZ with kids.

sections.heading: Air NZ Domestic Flights with Kids
sections.body: Air NZ operates multiple daily flights from major cities like Auckland, Wellington, and Christchurch. When booking, consider the route and time of day to minimize travel disruptions. For example, flights from Auckland to Queenstown take approximately 1.5 hours and cost around $150-$250 per person, depending on the time of year and demand.

sections.heading: Family Seating Options
list: Book seats together: Air NZ allows you to select seats in advance for a fee of $10-$30 per person
Consider the 'Skycouch': A row of three seats that can be converted into a flat surface for $200-$300 per person
Use the Air NZ app: To check-in, select seats, and access your boarding pass

sections.heading: Baggage Allowance
sections.body: Air NZ offers a standard baggage allowance of 23kg per person, with additional baggage available for purchase at $20-$30 per kilogram. It's essential to check the Air NZ website for the most up-to-date baggage information and to pre-purchase additional baggage if needed.

sections.heading: Tips for Flying with Kids
list: Book a morning flight to minimize travel disruptions
Pack entertainment for the kids, such as tablets or coloring books
Consider a 'Kid's Pack' for $10, which includes a meal, snack, and drink

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body: Once you arrive at your destination, consider booking family-friendly accommodation. In Queenstown, options like the Novotel Queenstown Lakeside or the Rees Hotel Queenstown offer spacious rooms and amenities for families. Prices start from around $200 per night.
